title: Electric Cars Around the Globe
content_html:
Electric Cars Around the Globe

Cars have reshaped our world since they first rolled off mass-production lines in the early twentieth century. One- and two-thousand-year-old Roman roads have been replaced by highways. Longer and wider bridges span rivers. The sharp division between urban and rural landscapes has been replaced by suburban sprawl, town and country linked by eight-lane expressways with stop-and-go traffic. Gas stations are everywhere. Countries with oil reserves are enormously rich and powerful. After a century, the romance with internal combustion engines is on the wane. As the price of oil rises, the reserves of irreplaceable oil are consumed, and exhaust fumes hamper life in urban areas, alternatives to gas-powered vehicles are becoming more attractive.

In the early twentieth century in North America, electric cars shared the roads with gas-fueled cars, but after a short time, gas-fueled cars became the standard. Although electric cars were quieter, cleaner, and easier to start, they were not able to travel the required distances, and their plodding speed failed to capture the imagination.

Lately, in Europe and in Asia, where commuting distances are shorter and gas is more expensive than in the United States, electric cars have grown in popularity. Electric recharging stations are appearing in cities. The government of China has offered monetary incentives to car manufacturers for each electric car they manufacture as well as to the people who purchase the electric cars. Taxi drivers in Tokyo have embraced electric vehicles. Major car manufacturers, including Mitsubishi, Nissan, Toyota, and Mercedes Benz, all offer electric cars everywhere but in North America.

In North America, slow, short-ranged electric vehicles with a high initial cost have thus far appealed to a limited audience. An American electric car that appeared briefly in the 1990s had a cruising speed of twenty-five miles per hour and could travel eighty-five miles on a single charge. Since then, battery technology has improved markedly. More recently, a North American company introduced an electric sports car that can travel 300 miles on a single charge and accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in 3.7 seconds, similar to the best sports car. The hope is that North Americans will embrace the new technology when they see an electric car as appealing as a conventional sports car.

Other American auto manufacturers are marketing electric cars as they do in Europe, as commuter cars. The design of many of these cars is innovative: Some are made of light composites and seat only two people. One is a three-wheeler that is classified as a motorcycle. Another electric car, the Tango, is five inches narrower than a large motorcycle and seats two, one behind the other. Four of these vehicles fit in a single parking space. The vehicle is marketed as a great way to drive between lanes of stopped traffic.

All electric cars will help to reduce exhaust and greenhouse gases; some will do it with greater flair than others.

questions.text: New ______ neighborhoods have developed between the cities and the rural areas.
questions.question: New ______ neighborhoods have developed between the cities and the rural areas.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–7

Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–K, below.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định từ loại

Chỗ trống đứng trước danh từ neighborhoods.

⇒ Cần tính từ.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 1:

"The sharp division between urban and rural landscapes has been replaced by suburban sprawl..."

Bước 3: Phân tích
suburban = thuộc vùng ngoại ô
suburban neighborhoods = khu dân cư ngoại ô

Đáp án: suburban

_blockConfig.noteContent: Cars have had enormous effects on the way our world looks. The landscape is now covered with highways and big bridges. New [1] neighborhoods have developed between the cities and the rural areas. Cars are also causing serious problems. Oil is expensive, and we have already [2] a lot of oil that cannot be replaced. Gas-powered cars also pollute the air with their [3] .In the early days, both electric and gas-powered cars were common, but people felt that electric cars did not have the flair that gas-powered cars had. For example, electric cars traveled at a more [4] speed. Gas-powered cars became more popular, and now they are the [5] .However, there is a renewed interest in electric cars, and they have been [6] by people in many countries around the world. Manufacturers are developing electric cars to sell to [7] in both Europe and North America, because these cars are a good way to get to work.

questions.questionHtml: Cars have had enormous effects on the way our world looks. The landscape is now covered with highways and big bridges. New 1_______ neighborhoods have developed between the cities and the rural areas. Cars are also causing serious problems. Oil is expensive, and we have already 2_______ a lot of oil that cannot be replaced. Gas-powered cars also pollute the air with their 3_______ .In the early days, both electric and gas-powered cars were common, but people felt that electric cars did not have the flair that gas-powered cars had. For example, electric cars traveled at a more 4_______ speed. Gas-powered cars became more popular, and now they are the 5_______ .However, there is a renewed interest in electric cars, and they have been 6_______ by people in many countries around the world. Manufacturers are developing electric cars to sell to 7_______ in both Europe and North America, because these cars are a good way to get to work.

questions.text: we have already ______ a lot of oil that cannot be replaced
questions.question: we have already ______ a lot of oil that cannot be replaced
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định từ loại

Sau have already cần V3/ed.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 1:

"the reserves of irreplaceable oil are consumed"

Bước 3: Phân tích
consume = tiêu thụ
consumed oil = dầu đã bị tiêu thụ

Đáp án: consumed

questions.text: Gas-powered cars also pollute the air with their ______.
questions.question: Gas-powered cars also pollute the air with their ______.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định từ loại

Sau tính từ sở hữu their cần danh từ.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 1:

"exhaust fumes hamper life in urban areas"

Bước 3: Phân tích
exhaust fumes = khí thải
pollute the air with their fumes = gây ô nhiễm không khí bằng khí thải

Đáp án: fumes

questions.text: electric cars traveled at a more ______ speed
questions.question: electric cars traveled at a more ______ speed
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định từ loại

Trước danh từ speed cần tính từ.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 2:

"their plodding speed failed to capture the imagination"

Bước 3: Phân tích
plodding = chậm chạp
plodding speed = tốc độ chậm

Đáp án: plodding

questions.text: Gas-powered cars became more popular, and now they are the ______.
questions.question: Gas-powered cars became more popular, and now they are the ______.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định từ loại

Sau the cần danh từ.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 2:

"gas-fueled cars became the standard"

Bước 3: Phân tích
standard = tiêu chuẩn phổ biến được chấp nhận rộng rãi

Đáp án: standard

questions.text: there is a renewed interest in electric cars, and they have been ______ by people in many countries
questions.question: there is a renewed interest in electric cars, and they have been ______ by people in many countries
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định từ loại

Sau have been cần V3.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 3:

"Taxi drivers in Tokyo have embraced electric vehicles."

Bước 3: Phân tích
embrace = đón nhận, hưởng ứng
have been embraced = đã được đón nhận

Đáp án: embraced

questions.text: Manufacturers are developing electric cars to sell to ______ in both Europe and North America
questions.question: Manufacturers are developing electric cars to sell to ______ in both Europe and North America
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Xác định từ loại

Sau giới từ to cần danh từ chỉ người.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 5:

"marketing electric cars ... as commuter cars"

Bước 3: Phân tích
commuter = người đi làm/đi học hằng ngày
commuter cars = xe dành cho người đi làm

⇒ sell to commuters.

Đáp án: commuters

questions.text: Car manufacturers are developing a_______of electric car
questions.question: Car manufacturers are developing a_______of electric car
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng sau mạo từ a và đứng trước giới từ of (a .......... of electric car). Vị trí này yêu cầu một Danh từ số ít.

Xét từ loại:

class: Danh từ số ít (dòng xe, phân khúc, hạng xe)-> CHỌN
classification: Danh từ số ít (sự phân loại - không phù hợp về ngữ nghĩa).

classify: Động từ.

Dịch nghĩa: Các nhà sản xuất ô tô đang phát triển một dòng/phân khúc xe điện đặc biệt dành cho...

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question: electric car especially for _______
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng sau giới từ for chỉ mục đích (especially for ..........). Tại đây, ta cần một Danh từ (mang nghĩa chỉ người số nhiều để phù hợp bối cảnh chung của đoạn văn).

Xét từ loại:

commuters: Danh từ số nhiều chỉ người (những người đi làm bằng phương tiện cá nhân/công cộng)-> CHỌN
commutes: Động từ chia ngôi thứ 3 số ít / Danh từ số nhiều (quãng đường đi làm).

commute: Động từ nguyên mẫu / Danh từ số ít.

Dịch nghĩa: ...đặc biệt dành cho những người đi làm.

questions.text: Their main purpose is to get the driver to and from work. Because of their small size, they______little energy.
questions.question: Their main purpose is to get the driver to and from work. Because of their small size, they______little energy.
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đóng vai trò là động từ chính trong mệnh đề, đứng sau chủ ngữ số nhiều they (chỉ những chiếc xe) và trước tân ngữ little energy. Vì vậy, ta cần điền một Động từ nguyên mẫu.

Xét từ loại:

consumer: Danh từ chỉ người.

consume: Động từ nguyên mẫu (tiêu thụ)-> CHỌN
consumption: Danh từ sự tiêu thụ.

Dịch nghĩa: Vì kích thước nhỏ, chúng tiêu thụ rất ít năng lượng.

questions.text: They are 11______ because they cost much......
questions.question: They are 11______ because they cost much......
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng ngay sau động từ liên kết are (They are ..........). Vị trí này cần một Tính từ đóng vai trò làm vị ngữ miêu tả tính chất của chủ ngữ.

Xét từ loại:
appeal: Động từ nguyên mẫu / Danh từ.
appeals: Động từ chia ngôi số ít / Danh từ số nhiều.
appealing: Tính từ (hấp dẫn, lôi cuốn)

questions.text: they cost much less______ than larger cars to run.
questions.question: they cost much less______ than larger cars to run.
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống nằm trong cụm so sánh chi phí cost much less .......... than. Từ loại cần điền ở đây phải là một Trạng từ bổ sung ý nghĩa khía cạnh cho động từ "cost" (tốn ít hơn về mặt nào?).

Xét từ loại:

monetarily: Trạng từ (về mặt tiền bạc, tài chính)-> CHỌN
monetary: Tính từ.

money: Danh từ.

Dịch nghĩa: ...bởi vì chúng tiêu tốn ít chi phí về mặt tiền bạc/tài chính để vận hành hơn những chiếc xe lớn.

questions.text: In fact, there is a______ difference in fuel costs between these new small cars and the larger cars that we are used to seeing.
questions.question: In fact, there is a______ difference in fuel costs between these new small cars and the larger cars that we are used to seeing.
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng trước danh từ difference (a .......... difference). Theo quy tắc cụm danh từ cơ bản, vị trí bổ nghĩa cho danh từ đứng sau phải là một Tính từ.

Xét từ loại:

marked: Tính từ (rõ rệt, đáng chú ý)-> CHỌN
markedly: Trạng từ.

mark: Danh từ / Động từ.

Dịch nghĩa: Trên thực tế, có một sự khác biệt rõ rệt về chi phí nhiên liệu giữa những chiếc xe nhỏ mới này và những chiếc xe lớn hơn mà chúng ta thường quen nhìn thấy.
